stemHtml: Ice crystals are often found in and around the anvil of a thunderstorm. If found in high densities at very low temperatures, what hazard do these present to an aircraft?

options.a: The crystals can accumulate on the aircraft's communication and navigation antenna systems and affect transmission and reception.
options.b: Because of their size, in high densities the crystals can reflect and block propagated satellite communication and navigation signals.
options.c: The crystals can accumulate inside the engine, then break away, causing a sudden loss of thrust, especially at low power settings.
options.d: The crystals can accumulate on the wing and tailplane leading edges, reducing lift and increasing drag.
